samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO implementation with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width parameter.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">FIFO depth parameter.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Define parameters
        parameter ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
        
        // Declare internal signals
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] raddr;
        reg wen;
        reg ren;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr_bin;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] raddr_bin;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] wptr;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] rptr;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] wptr_buff;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] rptr_buff;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] wptr_syn;
        reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] rptr_syn;
        reg wfull_reg;
        reg rempty_reg;
        // Convert binary to Gray code
        function [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] bin_to_gray(input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] bin);
            begin
                bin_to_gray = bin ^ (bin >> 1);
            end
        endfunction
        
        // Convert Gray code to binary
        function [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] gray_to_bin(input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] gray);
            integer i;
            begin
                gray_to_bin[ADDR_WIDTH-1] = gray[ADDR_WIDTH-1];
                for (i = ADDR_WIDTH-2; i >= 0; i = i - 1) begin
                    gray_to_bin[i] = gray_to_bin[i+1] ^ gray[i];
                end
            end
        endfunction
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Dual-port RAM instance
        dual_port_RAM #(.DEPTH(DEPTH), .WIDTH(WIDTH)) ram (
            .wclk(wclk),
            .wenc(wen),
            .waddr(waddr),
            .wdata(wdata),
            .rclk(rclk),
            .renc(ren),
            .raddr(raddr),
            .rdata(rdata)
        );
        // Write pointer logic
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr <= 0;
            end else if (winc && !wfull_reg) begin
                waddr <= waddr + 1;
            end
        end
        // Read pointer logic
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr <= 0;
            end else if (rinc && !rempty_reg) begin
                raddr <= raddr + 1;
            end
        end
        // Binary to Gray code conversion
        always @(*) begin
            wptr = bin_to_gray(waddr);
            rptr = bin_to_gray(raddr);
        end
        // Buffer registers for write pointer synchronization
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        // Buffer registers for read pointer synchronization
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        end
        // Synchronize write pointer to read clock domain
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                wptr_syn <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_syn <= wptr_buff;
            end
        end
        // Synchronize read pointer to write clock domain
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                rptr_syn <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_syn <= rptr_buff;
            end
        end
        // Full and empty signal generation
        always @(*) begin
            wfull_reg = (wptr == {~rptr_syn[ADDR_WIDTH-1], rptr_syn[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
            rempty_reg = (rptr == wptr_syn);
        end
        // Assign outputs
        assign wfull = wfull_reg;
        assign rempty = rempty_reg;
        assign rdata = rdata;
        // Control signals for write port
        assign wen = winc & !wfull_reg;
        // Control signals for read port
        assign ren = rinc & !rempty_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>
